questions.question_text: You have a Fabric workspace named Workspace1 that contains a warehouse named DW1 and a data pipeline named Pipeline1. You add a user named User3. User3 must be able to view all items in Workspace1 and update tables in DW1. The solution must follow the principle of least privilege. Which workspace role should you assign to User3?

You have a Fabric workspace that contains a lakehouse named Lakehouse1. A user must query table data by using SQL but must NOT use Apache Spark to query the underlying files and must not access other workspace items. What should you do?

options.text: Share Lakehouse1 with the user and grant Read all SQL endpoint data permission.
Assign the Viewer role and share Lakehouse1.
Share Lakehouse1 and grant Build reports permission.
Assign the Member role and share Lakehouse1.

questions.question_text: You must apply endorsement badges to the following Fabric items based on governance rules.
Evaluate the following statements about workspace, domain, and user permissions in Microsoft Fabric.

statements.statement: User3 has Viewer role access to Workspace3.
User3 has Domain contributor access to Domain1.
User2 has Contributor role access to Workspace3.

questions.question_text: You have two Fabric workspaces named Workspace1 and Workspace2 and a deployment pipeline that deploys items from Workspace1 to Workspace2. Some items have matching names and are paired. You need to ensure items in Workspace1 overwrite corresponding items in Workspace2 with minimal effort. What should you do?

options.text: Delete all the items in Workspace2 and run the deployment pipeline.
Rename the items in Workspace2 to match Workspace1.
Back up items in Workspace2 and run the deployment pipeline.
Run the deployment pipeline without modifying the items.

questions.question_text: You restructure Workspace1 by adding a folder and moving a pipeline into the folder. You deploy the changes by using a deployment pipeline. What happens in Workspace2?

options.text: The folder is created, the pipeline moves to the folder, and the lakehouse is deployed.
Only the pipeline and lakehouse are deployed.
The folder is created and all items move to the folder.
Only the folder is created and the pipeline moves.

questions.question_text: Ensure Python libraries are available by default for notebooks in Workspace1. Which three actions should you perform in sequence?

You have a Fabric workspace containing a lakehouse and a notebook that reads data into a DataFrame and writes the results to a Delta table. You need to consolidate the underlying Parquet files in the Delta table. Which command should you run?
You are monitoring execution of Fabric items by using Monitoring hub. You need to identify in which workspace a specific item runs. Which column should you view?
You have a Fabric workspace containing a warehouse named DW1 that is loaded by a notebook. You must identify which version of Delta was used when the notebook executed. What should you use?
